name: Worx WX292
description: 20V Brushless Impact Driver - Worx's brushless impact driver with 175 Nm torque and aluminium gear housing, part of the PowerShare 20V ecosystem

markets.comment: WX292 is primarily sold in Europe and Australia; North America uses WX290L/WX291L model numbers

multi-brand-system.comment: Worx PowerShare is proprietary; batteries work across 20V, 40V, and 80V Worx products only

max-voltage.comment: Single voltage tool, no dual-voltage capability

recommended-battery-ah.comment: Typically sold with 2 Ah battery in kit; compatible with all PowerShare batteries from 1.5Ah to 8Ah

battery-included.comment: WX292.9 is bare tool; WX292 kit includes 2x batteries

motor-type.comment: Brushless motor providing 50% longer runtime, 25% more power, 10x longer motor life vs brushed

speed-settings.comment: Single speed gearbox with variable trigger control

weight-bare.comment: Approximately 1 kg bare tool weight; marketed as lightweight design

compact-design.comment: Marketed as compact and lightweight to reduce fatigue and access tight spaces

led-work-light.comment: Integrated LED work light for low-lit areas

led-light-modes.comment: Standard LED work light, single mode

clutch-settings.comment: Impact drivers use impact mechanism, not traditional clutch settings

tool-free-adjustment.comment: Quick-change hex bit holder for easy bit changes

housing-material.value: Aluminium gear housing, polymer body with soft grip
housing-material.comment: Aluminium gear housing for durability and extended service life

all-metal-chuck.comment: Not applicable - uses hex bit holder, not chuck

rubberized-grip.comment: Soft grip for better grip and control

target-user.comment: Entry to mid-level tool suitable for DIY and serious hobbyists; brushless motor targets more demanding users

msrp-bare.comment: Approximately GBP 65-85 bare tool (WX292.9); approx $80-100 USD equivalent

msrp-kit.comment: Approximately GBP 120-150 with 2x batteries (WX292); pricing varies by region

street-price-bare.comment: Typical retail price January 2025; approximately GBP 65-85 in UK

warranty.comment: 3 year warranty with registration within 30 days; 2 years without registration; batteries 1 year only

release-year.comment: Released circa 2017; Amazon listing data suggests 2017 availability

pro-tool-reviews-rating.comment: Not reviewed by Pro Tool Reviews

amazon-rating.comment: Ratings not reliably accessible; primarily sold through Worx direct and regional retailers

home-depot-rating.comment: WX292 model not sold at Home Depot; North America uses different model numbers
